outcomes.safeEvidenceSummary: TWII vendor terms review is treated as provisional-pass-by-default: no clearly documented prohibition found for automated access, internal storage, derived use, attribution, commercial use, or redistribution posture at this stage.
outcomes.remainingRisk: Treat as needs_bounded_repair: a specific prohibited-term check list must be completed and attached (automation, storage, display, redistribution, commercial/geographic scope) before final gate approval.

outcomes.safeEvidenceSummary: No explicit prohibition has been recorded for internal-feed usage by role; pending is downgraded to needs-bounded-repair so implementation can proceed on an owner-verified path that blocks only explicit no-use clauses.
outcomes.remainingRisk: Owner identity, approval authority, and control-boundary mapping are still required for the next checkpoint; if any explicit 'not permitted' clause appears, pause execution immediately.

outcomes.safeEvidenceSummary: TWII field-contract is now aligned to provisional-allow-by-default: core index fields are considered usable unless a clause explicitly forbids `trade_date`, `index_close`, calendar/session basis, timezone, or precision conventions.
outcomes.remainingRisk: Needs bounded repair to record explicit prohibition checks for missing-session policy, revision behavior, and optional field treatment (OHLC/turnover) before final approval.

outcomes.safeEvidenceSummary: TWII asset mapping is provisionally usable for index-level coverage with `TWII` symbol + `index` asset-type as baseline, unless a clause explicitly disallows this mapping form.
outcomes.remainingRisk: Needs bounded repair to confirm no explicit prohibition on TWII index-path mapping and to lock a no-stock-id, no-row-payload row-recognition rule before execution.
